---
title: "Projects"
description: "Funded conservation and research projects across Tanzania's coastal and marine ecosystems"
---

```{=html}
<div class="page-hero">
  <p class="hero-tagline">Science in action — funded projects driving conservation across Tanzania's coast</p>
</div>
```

## Funded Projects

::: {.project-grid}

::: {.card}
### Pamoja Tuhifadhi Bahari Yetu
**Funder:** European Union | **Organisation:** The Nature Conservancy
**Period:** May 2026 – Present

A multi-partner EU-funded project designed to strengthen governance frameworks, improve institutional capacity, promote community engagement, and support research, monitoring and surveillance systems for coastal and marine ecosystems in Tanzania. The overarching goal is to improve environmental protection and biodiversity conservation.

**My Role:**
- Coordinate and lead fieldwork activities in Mtwara and Unguja North
- Design data collection protocols and oversee field data management
- Facilitate community and partner consultations (IUCN, WWF, WCS, Forum CC)
- Track workplan delivery against EU–IUCN and TNC requirements
- Coordinate fellows, interns, and consultants on programme objectives

[Marine Conservation]{.badge} [EU Funded]{.badge} [Community Engagement]{.badge} [Governance]{.badge}
:::

::: {.card}
### TNC — Strengthening Marine Co-management & Livelihoods
**Funder:** The Nature Conservancy | **Organisation:** Mwambao Coastal Community Network
**Period:** July 2025 – April 2026

Focused on strengthening fisheries co-management in Tanga and reef restoration in Pemba Island, Tanzania. The project supports sustainable livelihoods for coastal communities through improved marine resource governance and restoration of key habitats.

**My Role:**
- Support fisheries co-management planning and community engagement
- Coordinate reef restoration monitoring and data collection
- Facilitate stakeholder engagement across Tanga and Pemba Island

[Reef Restoration]{.badge} [Co-management]{.badge} [Livelihoods]{.badge} [Pemba Island]{.badge}
:::

::: {.card}
### Blue Action Fund — Socio-Ecological Resilience, Pemba Channel
**Funder:** Blue Action Fund | **Organisation:** Mwambao Coastal Community Network
**Period:** June 2023 – April 2026

Supported marine conservation projects aimed at increasing socio-ecological resilience through community-led management of key coastal ecosystems in the Pemba Channel, Tanzania. The programme focused on conserving the ocean and improving the livelihoods of coastal communities.

**My Role:**
- Led ecological data management and analysis throughout the project lifecycle
- Designed and implemented biodiversity monitoring protocols
- Produced funder reports and contributed to adaptive management decisions
- Trained community monitors in fisheries and reef health data collection

[Blue Economy]{.badge} [Community-Led Conservation]{.badge} [Pemba Channel]{.badge} [Resilience]{.badge}
:::

::: {.card}
### NORAD — Sustainable & Inclusive Coastal Marine Economy
**Funder:** NORAD | **Organisation:** Mwambao Coastal Community Network
**Period:** June 2023 – June 2025

Through NORAD support, this project actively engaged communities in Mkokotoni, Mtowapwani, Muwanda, Makoba, Mafufuni, Misufini, Bima Subuni, Boma Kichakamiba, Zingibar, Mwaboza, and Moa towards building a sustainable and inclusive coastal marine economy on the East African Coast.

**My Role:**
- Ecological data collection and analysis across community sites
- Supported community marine economy planning with ecological evidence
- Coordinated field teams and community-based data collectors

[Marine Economy]{.badge} [Community]{.badge} [East Africa]{.badge} [NORAD]{.badge}
:::

::: {.card}
### ECOFISH — Sustainable Marine Resource Management in Small-Scale Fisheries
**Funder:** European Union | **Organisation:** Mwambao Coastal Community Network
**Period:** June 2023 – December 2024

Part of the EU-funded 6-year ECOFISH programme (2018–2024), this component promoted equitable governance of tenure in small-scale fishing communities in Zanzibar and the NE Tanzanian coast — extending successful octopus closure pilot initiatives and improving policies for better fisheries management across Eastern Africa, Southern Africa, and the Indian Ocean Region.

**My Role:**
- Data collection and protocol design for octopus and fisheries monitoring
- Supported community governance planning with catch and effort data
- Contributed to regional data sharing and policy advocacy

[Fisheries Governance]{.badge} [Octopus Closures]{.badge} [EU Funded]{.badge} [Small-Scale Fisheries]{.badge}
:::

::: {.card}
### BIOEEL-TZ — Anguillid Eel Biodiversity, Tanzania
**Funder:** International / University Partnership | **Organisation:** University of Dar es Salaam
**Period:** March 2021 – January 2024

An interdisciplinary project bridging knowledge gaps on migratory anguillid eels in Tanzania — examining the impacts of fisheries and economic activities on eel populations. The project combined scientific data collection with public outreach, involving fishing communities and schools. A milestone for awareness-raising about unknown natural resources in Tanzania.

🔗 [meerwissen.org/partnership-projects/bioeels-tz/](https://meerwissen.org/partnership-projects/bioeels-tz/)

**My Role:**
- Fish biodiversity data collection and environmental parameter analysis
- Narrated and contributed to a scientific documentary on anguillid eels (part of MSc thesis)
- Facilitated community workshops and school outreach activities
- Presented findings at international conferences

[Eels]{.badge} [Biodiversity]{.badge} [Documentary]{.badge} [MSc Research]{.badge} [Community Outreach]{.badge}
:::

:::

---

```{=html}
<div class="cta-bar">
  <a href="impact.html" class="cta-btn primary">See Impact & Outcomes</a>
  <a href="media.html" class="cta-btn secondary">Watch Documentaries</a>
  <a href="skills.html" class="cta-btn secondary">View Skills & Experience</a>
</div>
```
